events.name: click
events.description: Fires when the user clicks (mousedown + mouseup) on an element.

events.name: dblclick
events.description: Fires when the user double-clicks on an element.

events.name: mousedown
events.description: Fires when a mouse button is pressed down on an element.

events.name: mouseup
events.description: Fires when a mouse button is released over an element.

events.name: mousemove
events.description: Fires continuously as the mouse pointer moves over an element.

events.name: mouseenter
events.description: Fires when the mouse pointer enters an element. Does not bubble.

events.name: mouseleave
events.description: Fires when the mouse pointer leaves an element. Does not bubble.

events.name: mouseover
events.description: Fires when the mouse enters an element or any of its children. Bubbles.

events.name: mouseout
events.description: Fires when the mouse leaves an element or any of its children. Bubbles.

events.name: contextmenu
events.description: Fires when the right mouse button is clicked, opening the context menu.

events.name: wheel
events.description: Fires when the mouse wheel is scrolled over an element.

events.name: keydown
events.description: Fires when any key is pressed down. Fires repeatedly while held.

events.name: keyup
events.description: Fires when a key is released.

events.name: keypress
events.description: Fires when a key that produces a character is pressed. Deprecated — use keydown instead.

events.name: submit
events.description: Fires when a form is submitted. Call e.preventDefault() to handle it manually.

events.name: input
events.description: Fires every time the value of an input, textarea or select changes.

events.name: change
events.description: Fires when the value changes AND the element loses focus. For checkboxes fires immediately.

events.name: focus
events.description: Fires when an element receives focus. Does not bubble.

events.name: blur
events.description: Fires when an element loses focus. Does not bubble.

events.name: focusin
events.description: Like focus but bubbles. Useful for delegated focus handling on a parent.

events.name: focusout
events.description: Like blur but bubbles.

events.name: reset
events.description: Fires when a form is reset via a reset button or form.reset().

events.name: invalid
events.description: Fires when a form element fails built-in validation on submit.

events.name: select
events.description: Fires when text inside an input or textarea is selected.

events.name: DOMContentLoaded
events.description: Fires when the HTML is fully parsed and the DOM is ready. Does not wait for stylesheets or images.

events.name: load
events.description: Fires when the entire page including images, stylesheets and scripts has loaded.

events.name: beforeunload
events.description: Fires before the page is unloaded. Can show a confirmation dialog to the user.

events.name: unload
events.description: Fires when the page is being unloaded. Avoid heavy work here.

events.name: resize
events.description: Fires when the browser window is resized.

events.name: scroll
events.description: Fires when the document or an element is scrolled.

events.name: hashchange
events.description: Fires when the URL fragment identifier (the part after #) changes.

events.name: popstate
events.description: Fires when the active history entry changes via back/forward navigation.

events.name: online
events.description: Fires when the browser regains network connectivity.

events.name: offline
events.description: Fires when the browser loses network connectivity.

events.name: error
events.description: Fires when a JavaScript error or resource load failure occurs.

events.name: unhandledrejection
events.description: Fires when a Promise is rejected and no .catch() handler is attached.

events.name: copy
events.description: Fires when the user copies content. Use e.clipboardData to modify what is copied.

events.name: cut
events.description: Fires when the user cuts content. Use e.clipboardData to intercept.

events.name: paste
events.description: Fires when the user pastes content. Use e.clipboardData.getData() to read pasted data.

events.name: dragstart
events.description: Fires when the user starts dragging an element.

events.name: drag
events.description: Fires continuously while an element is being dragged.

events.name: dragend
events.description: Fires when the user stops dragging an element.

events.name: dragover
events.description: Fires when a dragged element is over a drop target. Call e.preventDefault() to allow dropping.

events.name: dragenter
events.description: Fires when a dragged element enters a valid drop target.

events.name: dragleave
events.description: Fires when a dragged element leaves a drop target.

events.name: drop
events.description: Fires when a dragged element is dropped on a valid drop target.

events.name: touchstart
events.description: Fires when one or more touch points are placed on the screen.

events.name: touchmove
events.description: Fires when a touch point is moved along the screen.

events.name: touchend
events.description: Fires when a touch point is removed from the screen.

events.name: touchcancel
events.description: Fires when a touch is interrupted (e.g. alert dialog or phone call).

events.name: play
events.description: Fires when playback begins or resumes.

events.name: pause
events.description: Fires when playback is paused.

events.name: ended
events.description: Fires when playback reaches the end of the media.

events.name: timeupdate
events.description: Fires periodically as the currentTime property changes during playback.

events.name: volumechange
events.description: Fires when the volume or muted state changes.

events.name: loadeddata
events.description: Fires when the first frame of the media has finished loading.

events.name: canplay
events.description: Fires when the browser can start playing the media, though it may need to buffer.

events.name: error (media)
events.description: Fires when a media load error occurs.

events.name: pointerdown
events.description: Fires when a pointer (mouse, pen, touch) becomes active. Unified replacement for mousedown and touchstart.

events.name: pointerup
events.description: Fires when a pointer is released.

events.name: pointermove
events.description: Fires when a pointer changes coordinates.

events.name: pointerenter
events.description: Fires when a pointer enters the element. Does not bubble.

events.name: pointerleave
events.description: Fires when a pointer leaves the element. Does not bubble.

events.name: pointercancel
events.description: Fires when the browser cancels pointer tracking (e.g. device goes to sleep).

events.name: gotpointercapture
events.description: Fires when the element captures a pointer via setPointerCapture().

events.name: lostpointercapture
events.description: Fires when pointer capture is released.

events.name: IntersectionObserver
events.description: Observes when an element enters or exits the viewport. Not a DOM event — uses a callback.

events.name: MutationObserver
events.description: Watches for DOM changes: added/removed nodes, attribute changes, text changes.

events.name: ResizeObserver
events.description: Watches for size changes on elements. More reliable than window resize for individual elements.

events.name: animationstart
events.description: Fires when a CSS animation starts.

events.name: animationend
events.description: Fires when a CSS animation completes.

events.name: animationiteration
events.description: Fires at the end of each CSS animation iteration.

events.name: transitionstart
events.description: Fires when a CSS transition starts.

events.name: transitionend
events.description: Fires when a CSS transition completes.

events.name: transitioncancel
events.description: Fires when a CSS transition is cancelled before it finishes.

events.name: storage
events.description: Fires when localStorage or sessionStorage is modified in another tab/window.

events.name: message
events.description: Fires when a message is received via postMessage from another window or worker.

events.name: visibilitychange
events.description: Fires when the tab becomes visible or hidden.

events.name: fetch (AbortController)
events.description: Fires when a fetch request is aborted via AbortController.
